mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the federal funds rate reflects ongoing monetary tightening that raises the risk-free discount rate applied to equity cash flows, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC between monthly changes in FEDFUNDS and 20-day forward returns exceeds +0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or episodes with rising FEDFUNDS generate positive mean returns not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A cumulative rise in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.3 percentage points over three months signals weakening labor income and imminent consumption compression, driving negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of walk-forward instances, or the mean forward return during triggers is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in nominal retail sales and an increase in consumer prices squeeze corporate real margins and erode household purchasing power, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS and CPIAUCSL changes fails to produce a negative and statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in predicting 60-day forward returns over any 3-year rolling regression, or the interaction-driven signal underperforms the univariate RSAFS signal by more than 50% in Rank IC.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate exceeds 3.5%, sustained restrictive monetary policy reduces the present value of future corporate cash flows through elevated discount rates, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered episodes with FEDFUNDS above 3.5% produce positive 20-day S&P 500 returns in more than 55% of walk-forward observations, or the mean forward return during these episodes is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A decline in housing starts exceeding 10% from its prior three-month high signals contraction in residential investment and downstream durable goods demand, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: Triggered declines in HOUST are followed by positive 5-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of walk-forward instances, or the mean 5-day forward return during triggers is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained three-month rise in industrial production indicates expanding manufacturing activity and capacity utilization that lifts aggregate earnings expectations, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Rank IC between 3-month changes in INDPRO and 60-day forward returns is negative over any 3-year rolling window, or positive-signal episodes fail to generate mean forward returns significantly above zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: The joint occurrence of declining payroll employment and a rising unemployment rate confirms labor market deterioration that contracts aggregate income and consumption demand, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term of month-over-month PAYEMS changes and UNRATE levels fails to exhibit a negative and statistically significant coefficient (p > 0.05) in predicting 5-day forward returns over any 2-year rolling window, or the signal predicts positive returns in more than 50% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions, a quarter-over-quarter decline in real GDP signals an impending recession that triggers broad risk-off repositioning in equities, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: Negative GDP growth episodes in late-cycle expansions produce positive 60-day S&P 500 returns in more than 50% of walk-forward instances, or the mean 60-day forward return during these triggers is not significantly below zero (one-sided t-test p > 0.05).
